content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The ___ stood on the stage and spoke all of his lines without making a single mistake.
distractor_rationale: audience refers to the people watching, not performing on stage
channel is a television or radio broadcast medium, not a person who performs on stage
album is a music recording, not a person who speaks lines on stage

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The children laughed at the funny ___ characters on TV — the animals could talk and dance.
distractor_rationale: ceremony is a formal event, not a type of animated TV programme with talking animals
band is a group of musicians, not animated characters on TV
art is a general creative domain, not a specific type of animated TV programme

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: We bought tickets and went to the ___ to watch the new film on a big screen.
distractor_rationale: album is a music recording you listen to, not a place where you watch films on a big screen
book is something you read, not a place where you watch films on a big screen
channel is a TV or radio broadcast medium, not a physical place you buy tickets to enter

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The ___ clapped loudly at the end of the concert to show that they had enjoyed the performance.
distractor_rationale: band refers to the musicians performing, not the people watching and clapping at the end
actress is a single female performer on stage, not the group of people watching the concert
celebrity is a famous person, not the collective group of people who attend and applaud a performance

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The pop ___ released their new ___ last Friday, and all twelve songs were available to stream online immediately.
distractor_rationale: cartoon is an animated programme, not a music release containing twelve songs to stream
ceremony is a formal event, not a collection of songs released by a pop group
channel is a broadcast medium, not a music product containing twelve songs

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The young ___ painted colourful pictures of the city and sold them at the market every weekend.
distractor_rationale: actor performs in films or plays and does not paint pictures to sell at a market
celebrity is a famous person in general, not specifically someone who paints and sells pictures
audience is a group of people who watch or listen, not a person who creates and sells paintings

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The five musicians in the ___ practised together every evening so they could perform at the local festival.
distractor_rationale: ceremony is a formal event, not a group of five musicians who rehearse and perform together
cartoon is an animated programme, not a group of musicians who practise for a festival
book is a written publication, not a group of five musicians who rehearse together

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The awards ___ lasted three hours, during which prizes were given to the best films and performances of the year.
distractor_rationale: channel is a broadcast medium, not an event that lasts three hours and includes prize-giving
act refers to a section of a play or a performer's routine, not a formal prize-giving event
cartoon is an animated programme, not a formal event where annual awards are presented

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The journalist interviewed the ___ backstage after her final performance in the play, asking her about her years of training.
distractor_rationale: audience is the group of people watching the play, not the individual performer interviewed backstage
celebrity is a general term for a famous person and does not specifically indicate someone who performs in plays with years of training
artist typically refers to a visual creator such as a painter, not a person who performs in theatrical plays

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: She switched to a different ___ on the television because the documentary on the first one had already finished.
distractor_rationale: act refers to a section of a performance or a performer's routine, not something you switch on a television set
album is a music recording, not something you switch between on a television when a documentary ends
book is a written publication, not something you switch to on a television set

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The ___ stood on the stage and performed her role in the play in front of hundreds of people.
distractor_rationale: audience refers to the people watching, not the person performing on stage
album is a music recording, not a person who performs in a play
channel is a television station, not a person who stands on a stage

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: We bought tickets and went to the ___ to watch the new film on the big screen.
distractor_rationale: band is a group of musicians, not a place where you watch films on a big screen
cartoon is an animated programme or drawing, not a venue you buy tickets to enter
ceremony is a formal public event, not a place where films are shown on a big screen

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The rock ___ played their new songs at the outdoor festival, and thousands of fans sang along with every word.
distractor_rationale: actor performs in films or plays, not a group that plays music together at a festival
audience refers to the fans watching and listening, not the group performing on stage
celebrity describes a famous individual person, not a group that plays songs together

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The painter spent three years creating the large ___ that now hangs in the main hall of the museum.
distractor_rationale: album is a collection of music recordings, not something a painter creates and hangs in a museum
act refers to a section of a performance or a deed, not a visual work made by a painter and displayed in a museum
cartoon is a simple or animated drawing, not a large serious work created over three years by a painter for a museum

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The concert hall was completely full, and the ___ applauded loudly after each piece of classical music was performed.
distractor_rationale: artist refers to the person creating or performing the music, not the group of people sitting and applauding in the hall
celebrity describes a famous individual, not the collective group of people attending and reacting to a concert
band is the group of musicians performing the music, not the people filling the seats and applauding

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The children laughed at the funny ___ characters on TV on Saturday morning.
distractor_rationale: an album is a music recording, not a visual programme with characters
a ceremony is a formal event, not a TV programme with funny characters
a channel is the broadcast service itself, not the characters that appear on it

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The pop ___ played three guitars and a drum kit on stage at the concert.
distractor_rationale: an actor performs in plays or films, not as a group playing guitars and drums
an audience watches a performance — it does not play instruments on stage
an artist usually refers to a solo performer or painter, not a group with guitars and drums

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: We bought tickets and sat in the ___ to watch the new film at the local cinema.
distractor_rationale: a band is a group of musicians, not people sitting to watch a film
an act is a performance or performer, not the group of people watching
a celebrity is a famous person, not the collective group watching a film

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The singer released a new ___ with twelve songs, and it became very popular on streaming platforms.
distractor_rationale: a book contains written text, not a collection of songs released by a singer
a channel is a TV or online broadcast service, not a collection of songs
a cartoon is an animated programme or drawing, not a music release with twelve songs

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The famous ___ painted large, colourful pictures that now hang in galleries around the world.
distractor_rationale: an actress performs in films or theatre — she does not paint pictures for galleries
a celebrity is simply a famous person and does not specifically paint pictures for galleries
an actor performs in films or theatre — he does not paint pictures for galleries

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: She changed the TV ___ because there was nothing interesting on the first one she tried.
distractor_rationale: you cannot change an album on a TV — albums are music releases, not broadcast services
an act is a performance or performer, not something you change on a television
a ceremony is a formal event — you do not change a ceremony when watching TV

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The young ___ performed her role so well in the film that she won an award for best female performance.
distractor_rationale: an artist creates visual art or music — the word does not specifically describe a female film performer
a celebrity is simply a famous person and does not specifically describe someone who performs a role in a film
a band is a group of musicians — it cannot perform a female role or win a best female performance award

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The awards ___ was held in a large theatre, where the winners collected their prizes in front of thousands of guests.
distractor_rationale: an act is a single performance or performer, not the entire formal event where prizes are given to winners
a cartoon is an animated programme or drawing — it cannot be held in a theatre for prize-giving
an album is a music release — it is not an event held in a theatre where winners collect prizes

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The opening ___ of the show was a comedian who told jokes for twenty minutes before the main singer appeared.
distractor_rationale: an audience is the group of people watching — it cannot tell jokes as a performer before the main singer
a channel is a broadcast service — it cannot perform as a comedian before the main singer
a ceremony is a formal event — it does not describe a single comedian performing before the main act

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The concert hall was full of people who had come to enjoy an evening of ___ music by famous composers such as Mozart and Beethoven.
distractor_rationale: cartoon music is informal and associated with animation — it is not linked to Mozart and Beethoven in a concert hall
art is a noun referring to creative works — it cannot function as an adjective modifying 'music' to describe the style of Mozart and Beethoven
celebrity music is not a recognised genre — it does not describe the formal style of composers like Mozart and Beethoven

content.instruction: Use the word in brackets in the correct form to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: My brother wants to be an ___ when he grows up. (ACT)

content.instruction: Use the word in brackets in the correct form to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The ___ clapped loudly at the end of the show. (AUDIENCE)

content.instruction: Use the word in brackets in the correct form to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The gallery opened a new exhibition of ___ paintings from the nineteenth century. (ART)

content.instruction: Use the word in brackets in the correct form to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The band received an award for the most popular ___ of the year at the music ceremony. (ALBUM)

content.instruction: Use the word in brackets in the correct form to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Her ___ performance in the lead role earned her a nomination at the national cinema awards. (CELEBRATE)
